description_long: This object consists of three vertically oriented rectangular wall segments, each constructed from reddish-brown bricks arranged in a regular horizontal pattern. The bricks display a variety of earthy tones, including deep reds, browns, and some dark gray mortar lines, giving the surface a classic, weathered appearance. The wall segments are flat and thin, with no visible openings, decorative elements, or structural protrusions. The brickwork is consistent across all three segments, suggesting a utilitarian design typical of urban environments such as alleyways, building exteriors, or garden boundaries. The surface texture appears rough and uneven, characteristic of traditional fired clay bricks. The overall impression is of sturdy, functional barriers designed to delineate or protect spaces in a city setting.
description: Three flat, rectangular brick wall segments with reddish-brown tones and rough, weathered surfaces. The brickwork is regular and utilitarian, typical of urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing three brick wall segments side by side, highlighting the regular brick pattern and color variations.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the wall segments, with minimal visible surface detail.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same brick pattern and coloration across all segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the thinness of the wall segments, with only the edges and slight surface texture visible.
